note: Mathlib/MeasureTheory/Measure/LogLikelihoodRatio.lean:37 defines llr μ ν x = log (μ.rnDeriv ν x).toReal, the log-likelihood-ratio statistic itself; the LR *test* (comparing 2·llr against a χ² reference distribution under H0, e.g. via Wilks' theorem) is not formalized — no Neyman-Pearson/Wilks/hypothesis-testing content found anywhere in Mathlib (grep for 'Neyman', 'Wilks', 'hypothesis' turns up only unrelated tactic-hypothesis hits).
verify_note: decl confirmed at Mathlib/MeasureTheory/Measure/LogLikelihoodRatio.lean:37 (noncomputable def llr μ ν x := log (μ.rnDeriv ν x).toReal); Q585740 label/description confirmed as the likelihood-ratio hypothesis test (Wikidata); grep for Neyman/Wilks in mathlib4 returns zero hits, matching the note; match_kind 'related' (not 'exact') honestly reflects that llr is only the statistic underlying the test, not the test itself — no overclaim.
